goal: Produce three bake-plan options (conservative / balanced / aggressive) for one (sku, on_date, branch), each with expected waste, stockout probability, units sold, and a baker-language tradeoff narration.

outputs.name: options
outputs.description: Length-3 array, one per kind: conservative/balanced/aggressive. Each has bakeQuantity + outcome {expectedWasteUnits, expectedStockoutProb, expectedUnitsSold} + narration sentence.

constraints: All numeric outputs (bake quantities, expected waste/stockout/units sold) come from generatePlanOptions + simulation — never from an LLM.
Narration is deterministic baker-language scaffold (no LLM); Gemma quotes these sentences verbatim downstream.
Bake quantities are non-negative integers; rounding rules in rules.json.
Exactly three options returned, always in the order conservative → balanced → aggressive.
approval_note: Owner approval at commit-time is the gate that writes a bake_plan_decisions row. The harness never auto-commits.

evolvable_surface.option_quantile_mapping: Which quantile defines each option kind (e.g. conservative=q0.5, balanced=q0.7, aggressive=q0.9). The harness can widen or narrow the band based on observed operator preferences.
evolvable_surface.batch_rounding: Per-SKU batch size and minimum-batch rules (e.g. croissants in batches of 10).
evolvable_surface.narration_thresholds: The stockout-% and waste-units thresholds that trigger different narration clauses ('low chance', 'likely to run out', etc.). Tuning these affects how alarming the recommendation feels.

trace_emits: bake_plan_decisions (existing) — populated when the operator commits one of the three options. The committed option_kind is the learning signal for operator_correction vs operator_override classification.
